text: <par>We are assuming a Tamangic development of <latinform>*kr</latinform> > retroflex stop.</par>

<par>Note that the initial looks like an affricate in this reconstructed form, which would then go with etymon 704 <reconstruction>*zak ⪤ *džak</reconstruction> ARM/HAND. But all the daughter dialects have an initial j=/y/, which suggests etymon 696 *yak ARM/HAND. This needs to be rectified.</par>
<par>FROM M. Mazaudon: the initial is not an affricate, it is IPA [j], but the high series of tones in the reflexes indicate a proto voiceless so some prefix.</par>
<par>Chantyal (Tamangic) <latinform>chala</latinform> is a borrowng from Indo-Aryan. See also <latinform>kəpal-ye chala</latinform>, lit. "head-skin".</par>
<par>cf. <latinform>kuruŋ</latinform>, all <latinform>kruŋ</latinform> below, also kum below? any relsp. to 2119??</par>
